Pride & Prejudice (2005)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $44,780,310Details
International Box Office $88,175,175
Worldwide Box Office $132,955,485
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $74,269,058
Total $74,269,058
Further financial details...

Synopsis

When wealthy bachelor Mr. Bingley takes up residence in a nearby mansion, the Bennets are abuzz. Amongst the man’s sophisticated circle of London friends and the influx of young militia officers, surely there will be no shortage of suitors for the Bennet sisters. Eldest daughter Jane, serene and beautiful, seems poised to win Mr. Bingley’s heart. For her part, Lizzie meets with the handsome and—it would seem—snobbish Mr. Darcy, and the battle of the sexes is joined. Their encounters are frequent and spirited yet far from encouraging. But a crisis involving youngest sister Lydia soon opens Lizzie’s eyes to the true nature of her relationship with Mr. Darcy. The ensuing rush of feelings leaves no one unchanged, and inspires the Bennets and everyone around them to reaffirm what is most important in life.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$2,865,017 (6.4% of total gross)
Legs:6.26 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:33.7% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$28,000,000 (worldwide box office is 4.7 times production budget)
Theater counts:215 opening theaters/1,393 max. theaters, 7.8 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $77,832,362

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: November 11th, 2005 (Limited) by Focus Features, released as Pride and Prejudice
November 23rd, 2005 (Expands Wide) by Focus Features, released as Pride and Prejudice
April 18th, 2025 (Wide) by Focus Features (20th Anniversary)
International Releases: October 20th, 2005 (Wide) (New Zealand)
April 25th, 2024 (Limited) (20th Anniversary) (United Kingdom)
April 30th, 2025 (Limited), released as Orgullo y Prejuicio (20th Anniversary) (Mexico)
May 8th, 2025 (Limited) (New Zealand)
August 14th, 2025 (Limited), released as Orgullo y Prejuicio (Colombia)
Video Release: February 28th, 2006 by Universal Home Entertainment, released as Pride and Prejudice
MPA Rating: PG for some mild thematic elements.
Running Time: 128 minutes
Plot point: Eloping
Subgenre: Historical Drama
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Historical Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Focus Features, StudioCanal, Working Title Films
Production Countries: United Kingdom
Languages: English

Synopsis

When wealthy bachelor Mr. Bingley takes up residence in a nearby mansion, the Bennets are abuzz. Amongst the man’s sophisticated circle of London friends and the influx of young militia officers, surely there will be no shortage of suitors for the Bennet sisters. Eldest daughter Jane, serene and beautiful, seems poised to win Mr. Bingley’s heart. For her part, Lizzie meets with the handsome and—it would seem—snobbish Mr. Darcy, and the battle of the sexes is joined. Their encounters are frequent and spirited yet far from encouraging. But a crisis involving youngest sister Lydia soon opens Lizzie’s eyes to the true nature of her relationship with Mr. Darcy. The ensuing rush of feelings leaves no one unchanged, and inspires the Bennets and everyone around them to reaffirm what is most important in life.
